title: artemisia judaica subsp. judaica
intro: Artemisia judaica subsp. judaica, also known as Judean wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Judean wormwood include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Judean wormwood can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
